mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed solutions and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI allows you to manage your Linode resources from the command line.

```bash

curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This installs the Linode CLI on your system.
commands: curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli lke cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to your new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli lke kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure you replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary configurations for the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli lke cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure to manually check and delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes.

```bash

linode-cli nodebalancer list

```

> Check for any existing NodeBalancers that need to be deleted.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, drain nodes, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.conditions[] | select(.type=="Ready") | .status=="False")'

linode-cli lke cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check for any nodes that are not ready before proceeding.
> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are back to 'Ready' status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.conditions[] | select(.type=="Ready") | .status=="False")'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with your kubeconfig file or the cluster's API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate your kubeconfig using 'linode-cli lke kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create service: Service is invalid'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the service configuration is incorrect or if the Linode Cloud Controller Manager is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Linode Cloud Controller Manager with 'kubectl logs -n kube-system -l app=ccm-linode'.

troubleshooting.title: Node not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the node is not properly configured or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale your nodes or adjust resource requests/limits in your deployments.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved integration with Linode services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older node types and configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure to review the new node types and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x linode-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv linode-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
